title: Nostra aetate
content:
Nostra aetate () is the Declaration on the Relation of the Church with Non-Christian Religions <section begin=Modern criticism of the Catholic Church transclusion/ of the <a href="Second%20Vatican%20Council" Second Vatican Council</a. Passed by a vote of 2,221 to 88 of the assembled <a href="bishop" bishop</a s, this declaration was promulgated on 28 October 1965 by <a href="Pope%20Paul%20VI" Pope Paul VI</a.

It is the shortest of the 16 final documents of the Council and "the first in <a href="History%20of%20the%20Catholic%20Church" Catholic history</a to focus on the <a href="Catholic%20Church%20and%20Judaism" relationship that Catholics have with Jews</a ."

Similarly, "Nostra aetate" is considered a monumental declaration in describing the <a href="Catholic%20Church%20and%20Islam" Church's positive relationship with Muslims</a. It "reveres the work of God in all the major faith traditions." It begins by stating its purpose of reflecting on what humankind has in common in these times when people are being drawn closer together.
